authoritative_sources.title: FDA Food Code
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA Food Code is the recognized public baseline adopted or adapted by U.S. retail-food regulators and is authoritative for restaurant holding, contamination prevention, consumer advisories, and corrective-action framing that constrain delivery and takeout fulfillment.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: staging, holding, handoff-safety review, and exception escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Retail Food Protection Employee Health and Personal Hygiene Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA retail-food employee-health guidance is authoritative for illness reporting, exclusion, and hygiene expectations that change whether off-premises assembly, bagging, or handoff can continue safely.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: assembly safety review, contamination-sensitive exception handling, and escalation posture

authoritative_sources.title: ServSafe Delivery: Third Party
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ServSafe is the National Restaurant Association's recognized food-safety training framework, and its delivery course is a directly relevant public operating reference for packaging, transport, and third-party delivery handling in restaurant settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: order staging, packaging, courier handoff, and delivery terminology normalization

authoritative_sources.title: FTC, Illinois Attorney General Take Action Against Grubhub for Harming Diners, Workers, and Small Businesses
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this restaurant-delivery enforcement action directly addresses blocked guest funds, deceptive fee or availability representations, cancellation handling, and restaurant-listing consent.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fee complaint handling, order-status mismatch review, and cancellation or blocked-funds escalation

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. federal consumer-finance regulator for billing-error rights and is authoritative for duplicate charge, missing refund, and merchant-versus-issuer handoff boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch review, refund-not-posted handling, and issuer handoff packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions About California Senate Bill 478
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Justice, Office of the Attorney General
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California's Attorney General publishes the enforcement-facing public guidance for advertised pricing and mandatory-fee disclosure, making it authoritative for jurisdiction-sensitive restaurant delivery-fee and service-fee boundary handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fee-disclosure review, customer pricing complaint handling, and policy-conflict escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC's Red Flags guidance is an authoritative public control reference for suspicious identity and account-use signals that affect pickup-code misuse, rerouted delivery requests, stored-value abuse, and refund-redirection risk.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: identity-mismatch review, suspicious reroute handling, and fraud-sensitive escalation

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and scope grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the location, order, channel, guest, courier or pickup mode, current state, and whether the request is in scope, blocked, or owned by another lane before acting.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Order, payment, and dispatch truth-set review
workflow_stages.description: Validate order state, KDS status, dispatch milestones, courier assignment, pickup code, payment history, promotion impacts, and customer-contact records so execution stays tied to authoritative evidence.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Staging, pickup, route, and handoff control
workflow_stages.description: Track assembly, bagging, seal status, hold time, curbside readiness, courier or guest arrival, pickup confirmation, and route or drop-off handoff records before closure can proceed.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, failed-delivery, and service-recovery packaging
workflow_stages.description: Move unsupported refund asks, late-delivery complaints, no-courier events, redelivery requests, contact failures, and payment mismatches into explicit exception packets with the correct owner and evidence set.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Safety, fraud, and customer-harm stop-condition handling
workflow_stages.description: Stop unsupported actions and escalate whenever tampering, allergen exposure, contamination, blocked funds, suspicious identity, reroute abuse, or pickup-code misuse changes the safe answer.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closure, completion evidence, and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Confirm what counts as delivered, picked up, returned, remade, or escalated; record the next owner when needed; and preserve the public baseline when tenant or marketplace-specific controls are applied.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: orders, guest profiles, promised-time records, channel tickets, and pickup or delivery identifiers
KDS timestamps, expo and staging records, dispatch-board events, courier assignments, pickup codes, and curbside or handoff notes
proof-of-pickup, proof-of-delivery, signatures, photos, contact-attempt logs, failed-delivery notes, and return-to-store records
payment authorizations, settlement records, refund logs, blocked-funds complaints, promotion records, and fee disclosures
bag-seal records, remake tickets, item-availability and 86 states, substitution or allergen notes, and hold-time evidence
service-recovery notes, fraud or loss-prevention notes, customer-impact complaints, and escalation records
systems_of_record: service desk or complaint ticketing system
PMS or booking system when the restaurant operates inside a hotel, venue, or campus environment

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, rework-required, failed-delivery-sensitive, or owner-transfer sensitive.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed delivery, pickup, refund-boundary, and next-step summaries without overstating authority.
Prepare escalation packages and handoff summaries when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required order, payment, dispatch, pickup, delivery, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund-exception approval, legal interpretation, fraud adjudication, food-safety signoff, or courier-management authority.
A tampering, allergen, contamination, blocked-funds, suspicious-identity, or reroute-abuse signal changes whether execution can continue directly.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, franchise, marketplace, processor, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict.
refuse: Promise a refund, credit, redelivery, or fee reversal that the authoritative record and policy set do not support.
Override pricing, refund, safety, or fraud controls without explicit authority.
Mark work complete without pickup, delivery, or documented exception evidence.

domain_failure_modes: the lane closes a delivery or takeout case without proof of pickup, proof of delivery, or explicit exception evidence
a marketplace, POS, and payment mismatch is handled as routine status work instead of a blocked record-reconciliation problem
a late or failed delivery complaint turns into an unsupported refund or fee-waiver commitment
tampering, allergen, contamination, or employee-health signals are treated like ordinary service-recovery work
pickup-code misuse, reroute requests, or suspicious refund patterns are ignored in the name of speed
throughput pressure causes the lane to lose order-level traceability or skip contact-attempt evidence
the industry label is cosmetic and the pack collapses into generic restaurant operations instead of off-premises dispatch and handoff specificity

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State and local food-code adoption, franchise off-premises SOPs, marketplace contracts, contact-attempt rules, and fee or refund allocation rules can materially narrow the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses FDA, FTC, CFPB, California, and ServSafe sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve tenant jurisdiction, courier-partner rules, franchise policy, contact-attempt standards, and refund-allocation rules before tenant-specific outcomes are packaged.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure behavior instead of only generic restaurant or generic parcel scripting.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because tenant- and jurisdiction-specific differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.

The lane stops at delivery and takeout execution, exception packaging, and completion evidence. Pricing overrides, discretionary compensation approval, fraud findings, and final food-safety authority remain outside the lane.
